pi_items.source: setup_moodle.sh (line 323) creates course with shortname='BIO101'; task.json description references BIO101; setup_task.sh queries for BIO101
task.json description specifies Name: 'Biology Lab Notebook'; export_result.sh searches for LIKE '%biology lab notebook%' (line 60)
task.json description specifies 'Collaborative wiki'; Moodle wiki_mode field accepts 'collaborative' or 'individual' per Zoola Analytics moodle schema documentation; collaborative is default mode
task.json description specifies 'First page name: "Lab Safety Procedures"'; export_result.sh searches for LIKE '%lab safety procedures%' (line 103)
task.json description requires page 1 text: 'Always wear safety goggles and gloves when handling chemicals'; verifier.py checks for fragment in lowercase (line 116)
task.json description specifies 'Create a second page in the wiki named "Cell Structure Notes"'; export_result.sh searches for LIKE '%cell structure notes%' (line 121)
task.json description requires page 2 text: 'The mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell'; verifier.py checks for fragment in lowercase (line 131)

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified against task.json description, task scripts, and Moodle database schema. The task requires creating a wiki activity named 'Biology Lab Notebook' in collaborative mode in the BIO101 course (pre-created in environment setup). Two pages must be created: (1) 'Lab Safety Procedures' containing 'safety goggles' and (2) 'Cell Structure Notes' containing 'mitochondria'. All specified names and content fragments are consistent with task requirements and Moodle's wiki activity functionality.
